paper_title: ARTEMIS: A Neuro-Symbolic Framework for Economically Constrained Market Dynamics
human_review_reasons: Overall SIR confidence 0.64 is below the 0.65 auto-proceed threshold.
Paper-internal inconsistency: abstract claims 6 baselines including XGBoost; only 5 are ever reported (Table 2, Section 5).
Paper-internal inconsistency: DSLOB feature count stated as both 59 (Section 5.1) and 85 (Table 1, Section 3.4); Table 1's value used as authoritative.
Paper-internal inconsistency (found Stage 4): Time-IMM directional accuracy stated as 96.0% in abstract/Introduction but 86.0% in Table 2 for the identical ARTEMIS/Time-IMM cell.

Core architecture hyperparameters (latent dimension d_z, SDE step size/grid M, loss weights lambda_1..4, symbolic basis-library size K) are never given numeric values anywhere in the paper; only indirect hints exist in figure captions.

The paper's own Appendix A.4.1 explicitly concedes the Feynman-Kac PDE regularizer is theoretically incomplete ('not sufficient for full no-arbitrage, but provides a useful regularizer') -- a self-flagged scientific-validity caveat, not merely a missing implementation detail.

Stage 6 finding H-1: the repo's own default config (d_z=64, d_w=16) silently zeroes the market-price-of-risk loss (L_MPR) during training, since this loss requires square sigma (d_z==d_w). Should be fixed before any real comparison run.

Stage 6 finding H-2: DSLOB -- the dataset behind the paper's headline result and entire ablation study -- is generated from an unnamed source dataset (Section 3.4), making it structurally impossible to exactly reproduce regardless of implementation fidelity.
No user experimental results were submitted at any point in this pipeline run, so no reproducibility score could be computed (see comparison/reproducibility_score.json, score: null).

stage4_validation.method: synthetic/placeholder-data end-to-end smoke test (data/make_synthetic_seed_lob.py -> scripts/prepare_data.py -> train.py -> scripts/run_ablation.py -> evaluate.py -> scripts/plot_figures.py -> inference.py), since DSLOB's true seed dataset is unnamed/unavailable and Jane Street/Optiver/Time-IMM require user-supplied competition files
checks_performed: py_compile on all 40 generated .py files (pre- and post-fix): passed
train.py --dry-run for artemis: passed, 382,196 parameters built successfully, CPU fallback confirmed working when hardware.device=cuda but no GPU present

train.py --debug for artemis (full 2-phase: 10-epoch pretrain + 5-epoch symbolic distillation): passed end-to-end, including autograd-based Feynman-Kac PDE residual (Hessian via nested autograd.grad), market-price-of-risk loss, consistency loss, and Gumbel-Softmax symbolic layer

SymbolicBottleneck.export_formula() produced an actual human-readable distilled formula on real (debug-scale) trained weights -- directly validates this addresses the paper's own documented gap (no example formula ever shown in the paper)
train.py --debug for all 4 non-Chronos baselines (lstm, transformer, ns_transformer, informer): all passed, loss decreasing over 10 epochs
scripts/run_ablation.py for A1_NoSDE, A2_NoPDE, A6_MLP: all passed
scripts/plot_figures.py: passed after bugfix, produced figure2_drift_diffusion_profiles.png, figure5_prediction_scatter.png, figure6_ablation_losses.png (3/3 expected figures)
evaluate.py (both root wrapper and direct scripts/evaluate.py invocation, including the Wilcoxon significance-test code path): passed after bugfix
inference.py single-window prediction (point + conformal interval + symbolic-distilled prediction): passed

DSLOBGenerator full pipeline (detect_crash_window, fit_vasicek w/ 1.5x/1.2x amplification, fit_garch w/ amplification, generate_synthetic_features via VAR(1)+GP time-warp, validate_synthetic): passed, produced 460 windows of shape (20,85) from a 3000-obs placeholder seed series
Post-cleanup full recompile of all 40 .py files: passed

issues_found_and_fixed.issue: Both scripts ran the ENTIRE dataset through ARTEMIS.forward_pretrain() in a single forward pass. LaplaceNeuralOperator.forward materializes a [B, M, N, d_z, d_x] kernel tensor; with B=460 (full DSLOB dataset), M=N=20, d_z=64, d_x=85, this attempted an 8GB allocation and crashed with a CPU OOM (RuntimeError: can't allocate memory).
issues_found_and_fixed.fix: Both scripts now process the dataset in batches of 32 samples, concatenating predictions afterward. plot_figures.py's drift/diffusion profile sampling was also reduced from 256 to 32 samples for the same reason.
issues_found_and_fixed.verified: Re-ran both scripts on the full 460-window dataset after the fix; both completed successfully and produced plausible metrics/figures with no memory errors.

new_finding_not_in_sir_or_architecture_p.location: README.md (documented during Stage 4, not retroactively added to sir.json per 'do not regenerate completed stages')
new_finding_not_in_sir_or_architecture_p.description: A further paper-internal inconsistency was noticed while writing the README's 'Reported results' table: the abstract/Introduction state ARTEMIS achieves 96.0% directional accuracy on Time-IMM, but Table 2 itself reports ARTEMIS's Time-IMM DirAcc as 0.860 (86.0%). This is in addition to the two inconsistencies already documented in sir.json (XGBoost baseline; DSLOB feature count).
new_finding_not_in_sir_or_architecture_p.disposition: Documented in README.md 'Reported results' section with an explicit footnote; will be added to comparison/hallucination_report.md when Stage 6 runs. Not retroactively edited into sir.json/architecture_plan.json per this task's 'do not regenerate completed stages' instruction -- flagged here instead for Stage 6 to pick up.

artifacts_cleaned_post_validation: data/raw/*.npz, data/processed/*.npz (placeholder DSLOB test data, git-ignored)
stage4_validation.note: DSLOB is the only dataset with a runnable end-to-end pipeline in this repo, and even that uses a placeholder seed series (data/make_synthetic_seed_lob.py) since the paper's actual DSLOB seed source is unnamed. This validates code correctness/executability only -- none of these numbers are comparable to the paper's reported Table 2/3 results. Jane Street, Optiver, and Time-IMM preprocessors are implemented and importable but were not exercised end-to-end in this validation since they require user-supplied competition/collection files this pipeline cannot bundle.

stage5_validation.method: Executed both generated notebooks end-to-end via nbclient against a live Jupyter kernel (not just static inspection), using the same placeholder-seed DSLOB pipeline as Stage 4 (paper's real DSLOB data is unavailable).

notebooks_produced.purpose: primary reproduction notebook: environment check, install (+ path-injection fallback), paper overview, 5 component walkthroughs (LaplaceNeuralOperator, NeuralSDE, PhysicsLosses, SymbolicBottleneck, full ARTEMIS assembly) with toy-input demos, mini end-to-end fit cycle on placeholder DSLOB data, paper results comparison, next steps

exploratory notebook: drift/diffusion temporal profiles (Fig. 2 reproduction), PCA-projected latent drift vector field (Fig. 3 reproduction), DSLOB ablation study directional-accuracy bar chart (Table 3 reproduction), symbolic bottleneck distilled-formula bar chart (closes a gap the paper itself leaves open)

checks_performed: Pre-execution static compile() check on every code cell's source as standalone Python, run BEFORE attempting kernel execution (added after Stage 5's own bugs below were found) -- caught 0 errors on the second pass
nbformat.validate() passed on both notebooks after adding required cell IDs
Full end-to-end execution via nbclient.NotebookClient against a live python3 kernel: both notebooks executed with zero unhandled errors (after fixes, see below)

reproduce notebook: all 5 component demo cells produced expected output shapes; mini end-to-end block (placeholder data -> DSLOBGenerator pipeline -> small ARTEMIS fit over 3 epochs) completed successfully

explore notebook: all 4 planned visualizations rendered as image outputs (4/4 confirmed via output inspection); zero errors across all 13 cells; export_formula() produced an actual distilled symbolic expression on demo-trained weights
Confirmed both saved .ipynb files have execution_count=null and outputs=[] for every code cell (no pre-run output committed)
Confirmed no hardcoded absolute paths: both notebooks derive project_root via os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), '..')) relative to the notebook's own location

issues_found_and_fixed_during_stage5.issue: Build script erroneously inserted literal backslash-escaped single quotes (\\') inside f-string dict/attr access expressions (e.g. getattr(mod, \\'__version__\\', ...)), which is invalid Python syntax outside the outer string context -- a SyntaxError on first execution attempt.
issues_found_and_fixed_during_stage5.fix: Stripped all stray \\' occurrences from both notebooks' source cells (4 lines in the reproduce notebook); added a pre-execution compile()-per-cell static check as a new validation step to catch this class of bug before attempting full kernel execution.
issues_found_and_fixed_during_stage5.verified: Re-ran static compile check (0 errors) then full kernel execution (0 errors).

issues_found_and_fixed_during_stage5.issue: The demo reused Component 2's DiffusionNet, which was constructed with d_w=16 while d_z=64 (mismatched, deliberately illustrating the general non-square SDE case). PhysicsLosses.market_price_of_risk requires a square sigma (d_z==d_w) since Eq. 8's lambda(t)=mu/sigma is an element-wise ratio, so the demo raised an AssertionError.
issues_found_and_fixed_during_stage5.fix: Built a second, square DiffusionNet (d_w=d_z) specifically for the Component 3 MPR demonstration, with an added code comment explaining why d_w must equal d_z for this loss term to be well-defined.
issues_found_and_fixed_during_stage5.verified: Re-ran the cell: L_MPR now computes correctly (lambda shape [16,64], L_MPR=1.068535 on toy data).

finding_carried_forward_for_stage6.description: The Component 3 fix above surfaced a substantive default-configuration issue in the Stage-4-generated repo: configs/config.yaml's SIR-recommended defaults (d_z=64 per Fig. 3 caption hint, d_w=16 ASSUMED) are mismatched, meaning ARTEMIS.compute_losses() silently sets L_MPR=0 for every training run using the shipped default config (confirmed also present in Stage 4's own validation logs: 'mpr: 0.000000' appeared in the debug training output). This was NOT fixed in configs/config.yaml or src/arxivist_artemis/models/artemis.py per this task's instruction not to modify Stages 1-4; it is fixed only within the Stage 5 notebooks' own demo cells, and is flagged here for Stage 6's hallucination report to formally catalogue alongside the Time-IMM DirAcc and XGBoost/feature-count discrepancies already noted.
finding_carried_forward_for_stage6.severity_estimate: Medium -- silently disables one of the two physics-informed regularizers the paper's ablation study identifies as important (Table 3: A3_NoMPR alone drops DirAcc from 64.89% to 56.82%), meaning any reproduction run using the default config out-of-the-box is implicitly closer to the A3_NoMPR ablation variant than to the paper's A0_Full configuration.
finding_carried_forward_for_stage6.suggested_fix_for_stage6_or_later: Set model.artemis.d_w equal to model.artemis.d_z by default in configs/config.yaml (or make d_w a derived property rather than an independently-set ASSUMED value), so L_MPR is well-defined out-of-the-box.

artifacts_cleaned_post_validation: data/raw/*.npz, data/processed/*.npz (placeholder DSLOB test data, git-ignored)
/tmp/executed_reproduce_artemis*.ipynb, /tmp/executed_explore_artemis.ipynb (scratch copies used only for output inspection, not part of the deliverable)

stage6_validation.reason: No user experimental results were submitted at any point in this pipeline run. Additionally, DSLOB -- the dataset behind the paper's headline claim (64.96% DirAcc) and its entire 7-variant ablation study -- is generated from an unnamed source dataset (Section 3.4 never identifies it), making exact reproduction structurally impossible regardless of implementation fidelity. Jane Street/Optiver/Time-IMM are publicly available but require user-supplied competition/collection files not bundled with this repo. See comparison/reproducibility_score.json (score: null, requires_manual_review: true).
stage6_validation.hallucination_analysis_basis: architecture_plan.json + generated src/ code vs. sir.json + direct paper-text re-verification, independent of user results

key_findings: H-1 (Medium-High, newly escalated in Stage 6 after direct code verification): configs/config.yaml's shipped defaults (d_z=64, d_w=16) cause ARTEMIS.compute_losses() to silently set L_MPR=0 for every training run, since the market-price-of-risk loss requires square sigma. Confirmed via grep of artemis.py line 159 and cross-referenced against Stage 4's own debug logs ('mpr: 0.000000'). NOT fixed in this Stage 6 pass per 'do not modify Stages 1-5' scope -- documented only.

H-2 (High, paper-limitation not ArXivist defect): DSLOB's seed dataset is unnamed in Section 3.4, making the paper's headline result and entire ablation study structurally unreproducible by anyone outside the original authors.
H-3 (Low, already remediated): the Stage 4 OOM bugfix in scripts/evaluate.py and scripts/plot_figures.py, confirmed still present via grep for '# BUGFIX' comments.
additional_paper_internal_inconsistencie: XGBoost claimed as 6th baseline in abstract, never reported (sir.json ambiguities[0])
Time-IMM DirAcc: 96.0% (abstract) vs 86.0% (Table 2) -- newly formally catalogued in hallucination_report.md; recommend adding to sir.json ambiguities on a future re-parse
DSLOB DirAcc: 64.96% (Table 2) vs 64.89% (Table 3 A0_Full) -- already in sir.json ambiguities[4], reconfirmed as within seed-variance, not substantive

stage6_validation.next_step_to_unblock: Fix H-1 (set d_w=d_z in config.yaml) before any training run. Obtain Jane Street/Optiver/Time-IMM public data and run the full benchmark for a real (non-DSLOB) reproducibility score. DSLOB itself cannot be exactly reproduced under any circumstances given the paper's own data-provenance gap.
